title: alnus rhombifolia
intro: Alnus rhombifolia, also known as the white alder or Japanese alder, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its conical shape and smooth, gray bark. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Alnus rhombifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be used to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
